title: fraxinus americana var. juglandifolia
intro: Fraxinus americana var. juglandifolia, also known as the American white ash, is a deciduous tree native to North America. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its bark, leaves, and twigs, which contain compounds like salicin and flavonoids, displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Fraxinus americana var. juglandifolia include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and soothing digestive issues, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. Fraxinus americana var. juglandifolia is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
